sourceHighlights.summary: The patient has a history of CVA, Type 2 diabetes mellitus, hypertension, and right hemiparesis. No immunizations are recorded.

sourceHighlights.title: Diagnoses and possible coding support references
sourceHighlights.summary: Possible diagnoses include Hemiplegia, unspecified affecting right dominant side (G81.91), Personal history of transient ischemic attack (TIA), and cerebral infarction without residual deficits (Z86.73), and Other symptoms and signs involving the musculoskeletal system (R29.898).

draftNarratives.label: Homebound Reason
draftNarratives.draft: The patient is not homebound. The referral cites home physical therapy, weakness, right side hemiplegia, and history of stroke as reasons for home health care.

draftNarratives.label: Medical Necessity
draftNarratives.draft: The patient requires home physical therapy due to weakness, right side hemiplegia, and history of stroke.

draftNarratives.label: Patient Summary / Clinical Narrative
draftNarratives.draft: Tara Thapa, a 66-year-old female, has a history of cerebrovascular accident, Type 2 diabetes mellitus, hypertension, and right hemiparesis. She requires home physical therapy for weakness, right side hemiplegia, and history of stroke.

draftNarratives.label: Skilled Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: Physical therapy for weakness, right side hemiplegia, and history of stroke.

draftNarratives.label: Plan For Next Visit
draftNarratives.draft: Continue physical therapy for weakness, right side hemiplegia, and history of stroke.

draftNarratives.label: Care Plan Problems / Goals / Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: Problems: Weakness, right side hemiplegia, history of stroke. Goals: Improve mobility and strength. Interventions: Physical therapy.

draftNarratives.label: Patient / Caregiver Goals
draftNarratives.draft: Patient and caregiver goals include improving mobility and strength through physical therapy.
